title: Interface Flapping
symptoms: Interface repeatedly goes up and down (carrier transitions > 5 in 1 hour)
Intermittent packet loss on the affected link
steps: Check carrier transitions: 'show interfaces <intf> | inc carrier' — count how many in last hour
Check physical errors: 'show interfaces <intf>' — look for CRC, input errors, giants, runts
Check optical power (fiber links): 'show interface <intf> transceiver' — Rx power should be within -3 to -20 dBm
Inspect cable/SFP physically if accessible — reseat SFP if safe to do so
Check far-end: coordinate with the remote site/ISP to check their interface counters
Check for duplex/speed mismatch: 'show interfaces <intf> | inc duplex' — should be full-duplex
Enable dampening if flapping is causing route instability: 'dampening' command under interface
Review recent config changes: 'show archive log config all' for any changes in last 2 hours
resolution: Replace faulty SFP or cable. Fix speed/duplex mismatch. If ISP demarcation point: open ticket with ISP and request physical layer investigation. Apply interface dampening temporarily to prevent routing churn while physical issue is investigated.
escalation: If the interface is a core uplink (>1Gbps) or serves >500 customers and does not stabilize in 20 minutes, escalate to Network Team Lead and open ISP ticket concurrently.
reference: Emircom Physical Layer Troubleshooting SOP — Rev 2.4
